resume:
Wei Chen — Staff Quant Risk · 8 years

Experience
- 2021 - present · Meridian Capital (HK) · Staff Quantitative Risk Analyst
- 2017 - 2021 · ICBC International · Senior Risk Analyst
- 2015 - 2017 · PwC HK · Audit Associate

Education
- MSc Financial Engineering, HKUST, 2015
- BS Mathematics, Fudan, 2013

Skills: Python, KDB+, VaR, stress testing, Basel III, IFRS 9.

Languages: Mandarin, Cantonese, English.
jd: Meridian Capital APAC 招 Staff Quant Risk — partner directly with the Head of Market Risk on the HK desk. Scope: own the firm-wide VaR back-testing program across equities, FX, and credit books (~$4.2B notional), redesign the daily PnL attribution flow with the front-office quants, and lead one risk methodology project per half (current pipeline: switching from historical sim VaR to filtered HS, plus a stressed-VaR overlay for the China A-share book). Must-have: (1) 真正 owned 过 VaR methodology change at a regulated entity (not just supported); (2) 能 articulate model choice trade-offs (parametric vs HS vs Monte Carlo) without reading from notes; (3) 能 push back on the front office when their PnL doesn't reconcile — 这个职位 is not a support role; (4) 流利英文,Cantonese is a plus for the HK regulator conversations. Direct managerial scope: 0 reports now, 1-2 within 18 months.

history.q: Walk me through your current scope at Meridian — what books do you cover?
On the filtered HS migration you mentioned in the screening call — when did that project actually go live?
Who was the model owner of record on the regulatory submission?
Got it. 那 the design of the filtering window — GARCH(1,1) volatility scaling 还是 EWMA? Who picked that?
OK. 让我换一个角度。你说 partner with front office quants on PnL attribution — give me one example where their PnL didn't tie to your risk numbers and you had to push back.
你说 the difference was traced to a corporate action handling bug — 谁 first spotted that the numbers didn't tie?
Who actually did the reconciliation work — you, or the front office quant?
Right. Let's talk about the stressed-VaR overlay for the A-share book. What stress scenarios did you propose?
When you say 'we proposed the 2015 circuit-breaker scenario' — who is 'we'? You, your manager, or the broader team?
Did you write the scenario specification document?
OK. Let me ask differently — in the last 12 months, name one risk methodology decision where you were the person who made the final call, not your manager.
[after 8 seconds of silence] Take your time. One decision where the final call was yours.

candidate_last_answer: Sorry, 让我想一下…… [六秒静默] OK so one example — we, ah, the team had to decide whether to flag a large credit position as a stressed-VaR breach during the March 2023 SVB contagion week. The position was a basket trade and the historical-sim VaR didn't capture the regional bank correlation spike. So the team flagged it, we — sorry, the team — escalated to the CRO. I think 最终 the call was 集体讨论 reached, my manager talked to the CRO, the front office quant ran the alternative scenario, and we agreed to apply a 1.5x overlay multiplier for that week. It was a good team outcome. 我 contributed by running some of the numbers and 帮 my manager prep the memo for the CRO. Everyone played their part.

top_question_traits: expected-yield: who specifically chose 1.5x rather than 1.3x or 2.0x — forces candidate out of 'team' framing onto a single decision-maker for the magnitude
